---
title: "Clock QC and age-acceleration screening"
subtitle: "Clock-vs-age plots and RAA associations with maximum weight loss"
abstract: |
  This notebook provides per-clock visualization and exploratory association
  plots for the merged epigenetic clock table. It joins
  `data/AllClocks_Merged.csv` with three-timepoint metadata, calculates residual
  age acceleration (RAA) for selected clocks, and generates clock-specific plots
  of predicted epigenetic age versus chronological age using mixed models with
  participant random intercepts. It then tests and visualizes associations
  between each RAA measure and maximum weight-loss percentage using linear mixed
  models adjusted for sex, timepoint, neutrophil proportion, lymphocyte
  proportion, and participant ID. Outputs include combined PDFs for clock-vs-age
  plots, all RAA-weight-loss plots, and a selected RAA subset.
format:
  html:
    theme: sandstone
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, 
                      class.output="bg-light")
```

# Load 
```{r, results=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(tidyr)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(lme4)
library(ggeffects)
source("utils.R")
```

```{r config}
# ============================================================================ #
# Configuration: Clock visualization
# ============================================================================ #
FILE_OUTPUT <- "data/AllClocks_Merged.csv"
df_age <- read_csv(FILE_OUTPUT)
meta <- read_csv("data/meta_3timepoints.csv")

df_age <- df_age %>%
  left_join(meta %>% select(Sample, ID, Timepoint,
                            NEUTROPHILE, LYMPHOZYTEN, max_weight_loss_perc), 
            by = "Sample") %>%
  mutate(Timepoint = factor(Timepoint, levels = c("D-1", "D+10", "M+1")))

# Define clocks to visualize
clocks_config <- list(
  PCHorvath1 = "PC Horvath 1",
  PCHorvath2 = "PC Horvath 2",
  PCHannum = "PC Hannum",
  PCPhenoAge = "PC PhenoAge",
  PCGrimAge = "PC GrimAge",
  DNAmGrimAge2 = "GrimAge v2",
  PCDNAmTL = "PC DNAmTL",
  epi_age_DunedinPACE = "DunedinPACE",
  SystemsAge = "SystemsAge",
  AdaptAge = "AdaptAge",
  DamAge = "DamAge",
  CausAge = "CausAge"
)

# Age acceleration display names (for RAA plots)
ageaccel_titles <- list(
  PCHorvath1_RAA = "Horvath 1 RAA",
  PCHorvath2_RAA = "Horvath 2 RAA",
  PCHannum_RAA = "Hannum RAA",
  PCPhenoAge_RAA = "PhenoAge RAA",
  PCGrimAge_RAA = "GrimAge RAA",
  DNAmGrimAge2_RAA = "GrimAge v2 RAA",
  PCDNAmTL_RAA = "DNAmTL RAA",
  epi_age_DunedinPACE_RAA = "DunedinPACE RAA",
  SystemsAge_RAA = "SystemsAge RAA",
  AdaptAge_RAA = "AdaptAge RAA",
  DamAge_RAA = "DamAge RAA",
  CausAge_RAA = "CausAge RAA"
)

source("~/public/SiyuanLuo/projects/LongAge/R/CalAgeAccel.R")
ageaccel_clocks <- names(clocks_config)
df_age_accel <- CalResidAgeAccel(df_age, clock_cols=ageaccel_clocks, age_col = "Age")$data_wide
```

# Visualization functions

```{r plot-function}
# Plot epigenetic age against chronological age
plot_against_age_lm <- function(df, col_name, title_name, age_col = "Age") {
  model <- lm(as.formula(paste(col_name, "~", age_col, "+ Timepoint")), data = df)
  r2 <- summary(model)$r.squared
  r2_label <- paste0("R² = ", round(r2, 3))
  
  p <- df %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = .data[[age_col]], y = .data[[col_name]], color = Timepoint)) +
    geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.7) +
    # scale_color_manual(values = patient_colors, drop = FALSE) +
    # ylim(0, NA) +
    # xlim(0, NA) +
    annotate(
      "text",
      x = Inf, y = Inf,
      label = r2_label,
      hjust = 1.1, vjust = 1.5,
      size = 4.5, color = "black"
    ) +
    labs(
      x = "Chronological Age (years)",
      y = title_name,
      title = title_name,
      color = "Time point"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12, face = "bold"),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 10),
      legend.position = "right"
    )
  
  # Add fitted regression line
  p <- p + geom_smooth(
    aes(color = NULL),
    method = "lm",
    se = FALSE,
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "grey30",
    linewidth = 0.8
  )
  
  return(p)
}
```

```{r}
plot_against_age_lmm <- function(
  df,
  col_name,
  title_name,
  age_col = "Age",
  id_col = "ID",
  time_col = "Timepoint",
  line_mode = c("avg_timepoint", "reference"),
  reference_timepoint = "D-1"
) {
  line_mode <- match.arg(line_mode)

  # Packages needed:
  # lme4, performance, dplyr, ggplot2
  stopifnot(all(c(col_name, age_col, id_col, time_col) %in% colnames(df)))

  # Ensure timepoint is a factor (preserve order if already set)
  if (!is.factor(df[[time_col]])) {
    df[[time_col]] <- factor(df[[time_col]])
  }

  # ---- Fit LMM ----
  fml <- as.formula(
    paste0(col_name, " ~ ", age_col, " + ", time_col, " + (1|", id_col, ")")
  )
  fit <- lme4::lmer(fml, data = df, REML = FALSE)

  # Marginal R2 (fixed effects only)
  r2 <- performance::r2_nakagawa(fit)$R2_marginal
  r2_label <- paste0("R\u00B2 = ", formatC(r2, format = "f", digits = 3))

  # ---- Create prediction line (fixed effects only, consistent with LMM) ----
  age_grid <- seq(
    min(df[[age_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
    max(df[[age_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
    length.out = 100
  )
  tp_levels <- levels(df[[time_col]])

  if (line_mode == "reference") {
    if (!reference_timepoint %in% tp_levels) {
      stop("reference_timepoint not found in levels of time_col.")
    }
    newdat <- data.frame(
      tmp_age = age_grid,
      tmp_tp = factor(reference_timepoint, levels = tp_levels),
      tmp_id = NA
    )
    names(newdat) <- c(age_col, time_col, id_col)

    newdat$yhat <- predict(fit, newdata = newdat, re.form = NA)
    pred_df <- newdat[, c(age_col, "yhat")]
  } else {
    # Average equally across timepoints to get ONE line through all points
    newdat <- expand.grid(
      tmp_age = age_grid,
      tmp_tp = factor(tp_levels, levels = tp_levels)
    )
    newdat$tmp_id <- NA
    names(newdat) <- c(age_col, time_col, id_col)

    newdat$yhat <- predict(fit, newdata = newdat, re.form = NA)

    pred_df <- dplyr::as_tibble(newdat) %>%
      dplyr::group_by(.data[[age_col]]) %>%
      dplyr::summarise(yhat = mean(yhat), .groups = "drop")
  }

  # ---- Plot ----
  p <- ggplot2::ggplot(
    df,
    ggplot2::aes(x = .data[[age_col]], y = .data[[col_name]], color = .data[[time_col]])
  ) +
    ggplot2::geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.7) +
    ggplot2::geom_line(
      data = pred_df,
      ggplot2::aes(x = .data[[age_col]], y = yhat),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      linetype = "dashed",
      color = "grey30",
      linewidth = 0.8
    ) +
    ggplot2::annotate(
      "text",
      x = Inf, y = Inf,
      label = r2_label,
      hjust = 1.1, vjust = 1.5,
      size = 4.5, color = "black"
    ) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      x = "Chronological Age (years)",
      y = title_name,
      title = title_name,
      color = "Time point"
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
    ggplot2::theme(
      plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12, face = "bold"),
      axis.title = ggplot2::element_text(size = 10),
      legend.position = "right"
    )

  return(p)
}
```

```{r}
# Helper to fit LMM and return plot for one clock
fit_ageaccel <- function(df, outcome, cov="max_weight_loss_perc",
                         xlab="Max Weight Loss Percentage",
                         ylab=NULL, title=NULL) {
  # Use outcome name as default if not provided
  if (is.null(ylab)) ylab <- outcome
  if (is.null(title)) title <- outcome
  
  # data subset: require outcome present
  df_sub <- df %>% filter(!is.na(.data[[outcome]]))
  if (nrow(df_sub) < 5) return(NULL)
  formula_lmm <- as.formula(
    paste0(outcome, " ~ Female + Timepoint + NEUTROPHILE + LYMPHOZYTEN + ", cov, " + (1 | ID)")
  )
  model <- lmer(formula_lmm, data = df_sub)
  preds <- ggpredict(model, terms = c(cov, "Timepoint"))
  coef_est <- summary(model)$coefficients[cov, "Estimate"]
  pval <- summary(model)$coefficients[cov, "Pr(>|t|)"]
  label_text <- paste0(
    "beta = ", signif(coef_est, 3), ", ",
    ifelse(pval < 0.001, "p < 0.001", paste0("p = ", signif(pval, 3)))
  )

  ggplot(df_sub, aes(x = .data[[cov]], y = .data[[outcome]])) +
    geom_point(aes(color = Timepoint), alpha = 0.6) +
    geom_line(aes(group = ID), alpha = 0.3) +
    geom_ribbon(data = preds, aes(x = x, ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high, fill = group),
                inherit.aes = FALSE, alpha = 0.2) +
    geom_line(data = preds, aes(x = x, y = predicted, color = group),
              inherit.aes = FALSE, linewidth = 1.2) +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(
      x = xlab,
      y = ylab,
      title = title
    ) +
    annotate(
      "text", x = Inf, y = Inf, label = label_text,
      hjust = 1.1, vjust = 1.5, size = 4.5, fontface = "italic"
    ) +
    scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2", name = "Timepoint") +
    scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Dark2", name = "Timepoint")
}
```

# Epigenetic Age vs Chronological Age

```{r clock-plots, fig.width=16, fig.height=12}
# Generate plots for each clock
plots_list <- map2(
  names(clocks_config),
  unname(clocks_config),
  ~ plot_against_age_lmm(
    df = df_age,
    col_name = .x,
    title_name = .y,
    age_col = "Age"
  )
)

# Combine plots
pf_clocks <- wrap_plots(plots_list, ncol = 4) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") +
  # plot_annotation(title = "Epigenetic Clocks vs. Chronological Age") &
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"))

pf_clocks
```

```{r save-clocks}
# Save combined plot
ggsave(
  "figures/clocks_vs_age.pdf",
  pf_clocks,
  width = 12,
  height = 8
)
```

# EpiAgeAccel vs Weight Loss

```{r ageaccel-weights, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Build plots for each age acceleration outcome
ageaccel_plots <- pmap(
  list(
    outcome = names(ageaccel_titles),
    title = unname(ageaccel_titles),
    ylab = unname(ageaccel_titles)
  ),
  ~ fit_ageaccel(df_age_accel, ..1, ylab = ..3, title = ..2)
)
names(ageaccel_plots) <- names(ageaccel_titles)
ageaccel_plots <- compact(ageaccel_plots)  # drop NULLs

# Combine and save
if (length(ageaccel_plots) > 0) {
  final_ageaccel <- wrap_plots(ageaccel_plots, ncol = 4) +
    plot_layout(guides = "collect") +
    theme(legend.position = "right")
  
  ggsave("figures/AgeAccel.pdf", final_ageaccel, width = 13, height = 8)
  final_ageaccel
}
```

# EpiAgeAccel Subset

```{r ageaccel-subset, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# Select subset of clocks to display
subset_names <- c(
  "PCHorvath1_RAA",
  "PCHorvath2_RAA",
  "PCPhenoAge_RAA",
  "DNAmGrimAge2_RAA",
  "epi_age_DunedinPACE_RAA",
  "CausAge_RAA"
)

# Filter plots by names
ageaccel_subset <- ageaccel_plots[subset_names]
ageaccel_subset <- compact(ageaccel_subset)  # drop any NULLs

# Combine and save subset
if (length(ageaccel_subset) > 0) {
  final_subset <- wrap_plots(ageaccel_subset, ncol = 3) +
    plot_layout(guides = "collect") +
    theme(legend.position = "right")
  
  ggsave("figures/AgeAccel_subset.pdf", final_subset, width = 11, height = 6)
  final_subset
}
```
